data.reasoningText:
Now I have a full picture of the legacy project. Let me identify all the legacy patterns and modernize it to SDK-style format.

Legacy patterns found:
1. **Old-style `<Project>` element** with `ToolsVersion` and `xmlns` — SDK-style uses `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">`
2. **Explicit `Microsoft.Common.props` import** (line 3) — SDK-style handles this automatically
3. **Explicit `Microsoft.CSharp.targets` import** (line 46) — SDK-style handles this automatically
4. **Configuration/Platform property groups** with default Debug/Release settings (lines 4-14, 16-24, 25-32) — SDK-style provides sensible defaults


5. **ProjectGuid, FileAlignment, AutoGenerateBindingRedirects, and Deterministic properties** — all defaults in SDK-style that don't need explicit declaration
6. **Explicit `<Compile>` items and framework assembly references** — SDK-style automatically includes these through globbing patterns and implicit framework references
7. **AssemblyInfo.cs with manual attributes** — SDK-style auto-generates these, so I can move the metadata to the project file's `<PropertyGroup>` instead

Now I'm converting the TargetFrameworkVersion to the modern SDK format and building out the modernized csproj with the essential properties like TargetFramework, RootNamespace, AssemblyName, and metadata fields.

Writing modernized csproj...

Actually, I should trim this down—OutputType defaults to Library in SDK-style projects, and both RootNamespace and AssemblyName will be inferred from the filename `LegacyApp.csproj`, so those are redundant and can be removed. I'll also need to delete the legacy `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` file since SDK-style projects handle assembly attributes differently.

data.content:
Here's what was modernized — **47 lines → 12 lines**:

| Legacy Pattern | What Changed |
|---|---|
| `ToolsVersion` + `xmlns` on `<Project>` | Replaced with `Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk"` |
| `Microsoft.Common.props` import | Removed — SDK handles it automatically |
| `Microsoft.CSharp.targets` import | Removed — SDK handles it automatically |
| `TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2` | Converted to `<TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>` |
| Debug/Release `PropertyGroup` blocks | Removed — SDK provides sensible defaults |
| `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic`, `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects` | Removed — defaults or unnecessary in SDK-style |
| `OutputType`, `RootNamespace`, `AssemblyName` | Removed — inferred from project filename |
| Explicit `<Compile Include="...">` items | Removed — SDK auto-globs `*.cs` files |
| Framework `<Reference>` items (System, System.Core, etc.) | Removed — implicit in .NET Framework targets |
| `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` | Deleted — attributes now set via `<PropertyGroup>` properties (`Description`, `Company`, `Version`, etc.) |
